channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Panama Canal administrator pushes back against Trump's assertions of Chinese meddling
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — The administrator of the Panama Canal said Friday that the vital waterway will remain in Panamanian hands and open to commerce from all countries, rejecting claims by President-elect Donald Trump that the United States should take it over.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Venezuela latest: Maduro sworn in for third term as global backlash and sanctions mount
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro was sworn in for a third six-year term Friday despite international condemnation of his recent reelection as illegitimate, as his administration grows increasingly brazen in cracking down on opponents.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Venezuela's Maduro takes new oath amid protests and international rebuke
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro was sworn in for a new term on Friday, extending his increasingly repressive rule in the face of renewed protests and rebukes from the United States and others who believe he stole last year's vote.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US streamlines deportation flights to China
item.description: The Biden administration repatriated more than 500 Chinese nationals in the last year, laying the groundwork for systematic deportations across the Pacific Ocean.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Lynx on the loose in Scotland highlight debate over reintroducing species into the wild
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Scottish environmental activists want to reintroduce the lynx into the forests of the Highlands. But not this way.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Salvage complete of tanker at center of oil spill scare after being hit by Houthi rebels in Red Sea
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — An oil tanker that burned for weeks in the Red Sea after being attacked by Yemen's Houthi rebels and threatening a massive oil spill has been salvaged, a security firm said Friday.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Estonia steps up patrols in the icy Baltic Sea in a show of force after suspected cable sabotage
item.description: ABOARD THE EML SAKALA IN THE BALTIC SEA (AP) — As they plied the gray, icy waters of the Baltic Sea west of Russia on Thursday, the crew of the Estonian minehunter EML Sakala kept a careful eye on any vessels slowing down suspiciously or suddenly changing course.
item.dc:creator: EMMA BURROWS and HENDRIK OSULA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Greenland's leader says his people don't want to be Americans as Trump covets territory
item.description: The prime minister of the Greenland says the people of his mineral-rich Arctic territory don't want to be Americans but that he's open to greater cooperation with the United States.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Xi to send high-level representative to Trump inauguration: Report
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping is reportedly choosing a high-level official to represent him at President-elect Trump’s inauguration Jan. 20, in a break from diplomatic protocol of years past.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: US finds no 'Havana syndrome' link to foreign powers, but 2 spy agencies say it's possible
item.description: A U.S. intelligence report finds no evidence linking a foreign power to the mysterious “Havana syndrome” injuries experienced by U.S. personnel.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump setting up meeting with Putin, in communication with Xi
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump said he is working on setting up a meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin for after his Jan. 20 inauguration and is in "communication" with Chinese President Xi Jinping.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Puerto Rico's chief for natural resources shelves investigation targeting governor's in-laws
item.description: Puerto Rico’s new natural resources secretary has shelved an investigation into allegations of illegal construction in a protected area on the island’s southwest coast.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DHS extends TPS for Salvadorans, potentially complicating Trump's plans
item.description: The Biden administration on Friday extended a key humanitarian program for El Salvadoran nationals in a move that could complicate the incoming Trump administration’s plans to do away with long-term immigrant protections.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Biden levies new sanctions against Russian energy sector, but it's up to Trump whether to keep them
item.description: President Joe Biden's administration says it's expanding sanctions against Russia's critically important energy sector over its war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Latest: Supreme Court seems likely to uphold a law that could ban TikTok in the US on Jan. 19
item.description: The Supreme Court seemed likely to uphold a law that would ban TikTok in the United States beginning Jan. 19 unless the popular social media program is sold by its China-based parent company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel launches new strikes on Yemen’s Houthi rebels
item.description: Israel’s military says it has carried out new airstrikes on Yemen against what it said were Houthi rebel targets.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: An albatross couple shares egg duty in this captivating low-drama reality show
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — It’s a reality show about a loving couple waiting to welcome their new arrival, watched by thousands of ardent fans.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK foreign policy chief says Trump is right to urge higher military spending from NATO
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Europe’s security “is on a knife-edge” and President-elect Donald Trump is right to say NATO member nations must increase military spending, Britain’s top diplomat said Thursday. Foreign Secretary David Lammy said that “the post-Cold War peace is well and truly over.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Panama marks Martyrs' Day as Trump threatens to retake control of Panama Canal
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — A Panamanian national holiday remembering the killing of 21 protesters by police and U.S. troops in 1964 is taking on new resonance this year as U.S. President-elect Donald Trump threatens to take back control of the Panama Canal. On Jan. 9, 1964, students protested in the then-U.
item.dc:creator: JUAN ZAMORANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trudeau: Trump talk of annexing Canada a distraction, won't happen
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Thursday he thinks President-elect Trump’s public musings about annexing the neighbor to the north is a distraction from Trump’s threat to impose steep tariffs on Canadian imports.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader detained ahead of Maduro's inauguration
item.description: Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado was detained and later released amid antigovernment protests in Caracas on Thursday, a day before President Nicolás Maduro is expected to be sworn in to a third full term in office.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Musk uses X livestream to amplify German far-right leader's views ahead of an election
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — Tech billionaire Elon Musk livestreamed his chat with a leader of Germany's far-right party on Thursday, using the power of his social media platform, X, to amplify the party's message ahead of an upcoming national election — and raising concerns across Europe about the world's
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Poland adopts resolution protecting Netanyahu from arrest if he attends Auschwitz liberation commemoration
item.description: Poland passed a resolution to ensure the safe participation of Israeli representatives who attend 80th anniversary commemorations of the liberation of Auschwitz-Birkenau.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NOAA says La Nina ocean cooling has finally arrived, but it's weak and may cause fewer problems
item.description: A long-awaited La Nina has finally appeared, but the periodic cooling of Pacific Ocean waters is weak and unlikely to cause as many weather problems as usual, meteorologists said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An army airstrike on a village in western Myanmar has killed at least 40 people, reports say
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — An airstrike by Myanmar’s army on a village under the control of an armed ethnic minority group killed about 40 people and injured at least 20 others, officials of the group and a local charity said Thursday. They said hundreds of houses burned in a fire triggered by the bombing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mozambique opposition leader returns from self-imposed exile as police fire tear gas at supporters
item.description: MAPUTO, Mozambique (AP) — Mozambique's main opposition leader returned from self-imposed exile on Thursday clutching a bible and saying he still rejects the results of a disputed election last year that has sparked more than two months of protests and a violent crackdown by security forces.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES MANGWIRO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 Americans arrested in Venezuela on eve of Maduro inauguration over ‘terrorism’ claims
item.description: Two Americans have been arrested in Venezuela, with President Nicolas Maduro claiming the charges relate to an intent to "practice terrorism against the Venezuelan people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italian journalist Cecilia Sala freed from detention in Iran
item.description: Italian journalist Cecilia Sala was released after being detained in Iran for three weeks. Sala's plight became intertwined with that of an Iranian engineer wanted by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump reinforces 'all hell will break out' if hostages not returned by inauguration
item.description: President-elect Trump once again threatened "all hell will break out" if the hostages in Gaza are not freed by the time he enters office, though he refused to detail what actions he will take.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran's nuclear program is nearing 'the point of no return,' France's Macron says
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron is warning that Iran's nuclear program is approaching the "point of no return," calling it a top "security challenge.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jean-Marie Le Pen, French far-right leader known for fiery rhetoric against immigration, dies at 96
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Jean-Marie Le Pen, the founder of France’s far-right National Front who was known for fiery rhetoric against immigration and multiculturalism that earned him both staunch supporters and widespread condemnation, died Tuesday. He was 96.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, SYLVIE CORBET and ELAINE GANLEY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau to announce resignation: Reports
item.description: It remains unclear whether Justin Trudeau will leave immediately or stay on as prime minister until a new leader is selected, the report added.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: China fires shot across Trump's bow with defense sanctions
item.description: It could be a sign of a brewing tit-for-tat as the two superpowers jockey for economic and military leverage.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation
